quizQuestion:
Era necesario que {___} a tiempo.
(It was necessary that we arrive on time.)
sentenceTranslation: It was necessary that we arrive on time.
explanation: llegásemos — imperfect subjunctive -se form of llegar (nosotros). The nosotros form has a written accent: llegásemos. This is the literary -se alternative to llegáramos.

quizQuestion:
Era mejor que {___} más claramente.
(It was better that we speak more clearly.)
sentenceTranslation: It was better that we speak more clearly.
explanation: hablásemos — imperfect subjunctive -se form of hablar (nosotros). Written accent on á: hablásemos. The -se paradigm nosotros form always has a written accent on the penultimate syllable.

quizQuestion:
Era bueno que {___} más verduras.
(It was good that we eat more vegetables.)
sentenceTranslation: It was good that we eat more vegetables.
explanation: comiésemos — imperfect subjunctive -se form of comer (nosotros). Written accent on é: comiésemos. The -se nosotros form: comiésemos.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} más sobre historia.
(She wanted you to learn more about history.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted you to learn more about history.
explanation: aprendieses — imperfect subjunctive -se form of aprender (tú). After quería que (past volition), use imperfect subjunctive. The tú -se form: aprendieses.

correctAnswer: Si hablaras con él, lo entenderías.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hablases con él, lo entenderías.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If you talked to him, you would understand.
sentenceTranslation: If you talked to him, you would understand.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 2: si hablaras... + lo entenderías
explanation: Si hablaras con él, lo entenderías. Type 2 hypothetical: si + imperfect subjunctive (hablaras) + conditional (entenderías). This describes a hypothetical present: if you were to talk to him (but you're not).
targetLanguageWord: Si hablaras con él, lo entenderías.

correctAnswer: Si hubiera tenido tiempo, lo habría hecho.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hubiese tenido tiempo, lo habría hecho.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 3 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If I had had time, I would have done it.
sentenceTranslation: If I had had time, I would have done it.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 3: si hubiera tenido... lo habría hecho
explanation: Si hubiera tenido tiempo, lo habría hecho. Type 3: si + pluperfect subj (hubiera tenido — I didn't have time) + conditional perfect (habría hecho — I would have done it). Tenido = past participle of tener.
targetLanguageWord: Si hubiera tenido tiempo, lo habría hecho.

correctAnswer: Si no hubiera nevado ayer, haría menos frío hoy.
quizQuestion:
Complete the mixed conditional: '{___}'
Meaning: If it hadn't snowed yesterday, it would be less cold today.
sentenceTranslation: If it hadn't snowed yesterday, it would be less cold today.
grammarPointLabel: mixed conditional: si no hubiera nevado (past) + haría menos frío (present)
explanation: Si no hubiera nevado ayer, haría menos frío hoy. Mixed conditional: si + pluperfect subjunctive (past weather event that DID happen) + conditional simple (present consequence: it's cold now because it snowed).
targetLanguageWord: Si no hubiera nevado ayer, haría menos frío hoy.

correctAnswer: Si no hubiera venido, no te habría conocido.
quizQuestion:
Complete the mixed conditional: '{___}'
Meaning: If I hadn't come, I wouldn't have met you.
sentenceTranslation: If I hadn't come, I wouldn't have met you.
grammarPointLabel: mixed conditional: si no hubiera venido (past) + no te habría conocido (past result)
explanation: Si no hubiera venido, no te habría conocido. This is a type 3 si-clause (both past), but demonstrating how past counterfactuals can affect the present narrative. Type 3 result in conditional perfect.
targetLanguageWord: Si no hubiera venido, no te habría conocido.

quizQuestion:
{___} apartamento amueblado en el centro.
(Furnished apartment for rent in the center.)
[Singular subject = singular verb]
sentenceTranslation: Furnished apartment for rent in the center.
explanation: Se alquila — se-passive with singular subject. Un apartamento = singular → alquila (singular). This is the classic rental advertisement pattern in Spanish. Contrast: se alquilan pisos (plural).

quizQuestion:
Los acuerdos de paz {___} ayer.
(The peace agreements were signed yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: The peace agreements were signed yesterday.
explanation: se firmaron — se-passive in preterite. Los acuerdos (pl.) → firmaron (pl.). This is equivalent to 'fueron firmados' but more natural in everyday Spanish.

quizQuestion:
Le rogó que {___} un poco más.
(Direct speech: '¡Quédate!' She begged him to stay a little longer.)
sentenceTranslation: She begged him to stay a little longer.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech: rogar + imperfect subjunctive (quédate → se quedara)
explanation: se quedara — rogar que + imperfect subjunctive. The reflexive imperative 'quédate' becomes 'que se quedara' in reported speech. Note the pronoun changes: te → se (to match él).

quizQuestion:
Lo explicó claramente, {___} todos lo comprendieron.
(She explained it clearly, so that everyone understood.)
sentenceTranslation: She explained it clearly, so that everyone understood.
grammarPointLabel: consecutive connector: de manera que (so that / with the result that)
explanation: de manera que — 'so that / in such a way that'. With indicative (todos comprendieron = they understood = real result). Synonymous with de modo que + indicative.

quizQuestion:
{___} vivas en mi casa, cumple las normas.
(While you live in my house, follow the rules.)
[Hint: ongoing future condition — subjunctive required]
sentenceTranslation: While you live in my house, follow the rules.
grammarPointLabel: temporal connector: mientras + subjunctive (while — ongoing future/conditional)
explanation: mientras — 'while / as long as'. With subjunctive when it expresses an ongoing future condition: mientras vivas (as long as you live = future/conditional). With indicative: mientras estudiaba (while he was studying = past fact).

quizQuestion:
{___} seas mayor, lo entenderás.
(When you are older, you will understand it.)
[Hint: future event — subjunctive follows]
sentenceTranslation: When you are older, you will understand it.
grammarPointLabel: temporal connector: cuando + subjunctive (future event — classic example)
explanation: cuando — 'when' + subjunctive for future events. Cuando seas mayor = when you are older (future). The indicative would refer to a habit or past: cuando eras mayor = when you were older.
